ChunkingConfig.description:
Configuration for chunked (temporal) drift analysis.

When present, the test data is split into sequential chunks and each
chunk is tested for drift independently.  This answers "when did drift
start?" and "did it recover?".

chunk_size.description: Fixed number of samples per chunk. Mutually exclusive with chunk_count.
chunk_size.title: Chunk Size

chunk_count.description: Split test data into this many equal chunks. Mutually exclusive with chunk_size.
chunk_count.title: Chunk Count

incomplete.description: How to handle a final chunk smaller than chunk_size. 'keep': retain as-is, 'drop': discard, 'append': merge into last full chunk.
incomplete.title: Incomplete

threshold_multiplier.description: Z-score multiplier for the chunk-level drift threshold. Lower values are more sensitive (e.g. 2.0), higher values more conservative.
threshold_multiplier.title: Threshold Multiplier

ChunkingConfig.title: ChunkingConfig

DataCleaningHealthThresholds.description:
Configurable warning thresholds for data cleaning health status.

Each threshold is a percentage (0–100). When the detected rate exceeds the
threshold the corresponding finding is elevated to ``severity="warning"``;
otherwise it stays at ``severity="info"``.

Set a threshold to ``None`` to disable the warning for that metric.

exact_duplicates.description: Max allowable % of images in exact-duplicate groups. Exact duplicates are byte-identical images that inflate dataset size without adding information. Default 0% — any exact duplicates trigger a warning. Raise above 0 only if your pipeline intentionally includes repeated images (e.g. augmentation-before-split workflows).
exact_duplicates.title: Exact Duplicates

near_duplicates.description: Max allowable % of images in near-duplicate groups. Near duplicates are visually similar images (crops, resizes, minor edits) that can bias model training toward repeated content. Default 5%. Lower to 1–2% for curated benchmarks; raise to 10–15% for large-scale web-scraped datasets where some redundancy is expected.
near_duplicates.title: Near Duplicates

image_outliers.description: Max allowable % of images flagged as statistical outliers (unusual dimensions, brightness, entropy, or visual statistics). Default 3%. Lower to 1% for safety-critical datasets; raise to 5–10% for diverse real-world collections where high visual variance is expected.
image_outliers.title: Image Outliers

target_outliers.description: Max allowable % of targets (labels/annotations) flagged as outliers (unusual bounding-box sizes, aspect ratios, or annotation counts). Default 3%. Lower to 1% for annotation-quality audits; raise to 5–10% for datasets with naturally high annotation variance (e.g. dense object detection).
target_outliers.title: Target Outliers

classwise_outliers.description: Max allowable % of items flagged as outliers within any single class. Default 3%. This catches classes where outlier concentration is disproportionately high, which may indicate labeling errors or class definition issues. Lower to 1% for label-quality audits; raise to 5–10% for classes with inherently high visual diversity.
classwise_outliers.title: Classwise Outliers

class_label_imbalance.description: Max allowable ratio between the largest and smallest class counts (max_class / min_class). Default 5:1. For binary classification, 3:1 is a common threshold for 'imbalanced'. For large class hierarchies (25+ classes), the long tail naturally increases this ratio — raise to 10–20:1 to avoid false warnings. Set to 1.0 to require perfectly balanced classes.
class_label_imbalance.title: Class Label Imbalance

DataCleaningHealthThresholds.title: DataCleaningHealthThresholds

DataCleaningWorkflowConfig.description:
Typed workflow configuration for ``data-cleaning``.

Inherits all fields from :class:`DataCleaningParameters` — no ``params``
nesting required.

Example YAML::

    workflows:
      - name: clean_zscore_stats
        type: data-cleaning
        outlier_method: zscore
        outlier_flags: [pixel, visual]

mode.description: advisory: report only, preparatory: modify dataset
mode.title: Mode

outlier_flags.description: Image statistics groups for outlier detection. At least one required.
outlier_flags.title: Outlier Flags

outlier_threshold.description: Custom threshold (None = use DataEval default for chosen method)
outlier_threshold.title: Outlier Threshold

outlier_cluster_threshold.description: Std devs from cluster center to flag as outlier (requires extractor). None = skip cluster detection.
outlier_cluster_threshold.title: Outlier Cluster Threshold

outlier_cluster_algorithm.description: Clustering algorithm for cluster-based outlier detection.
outlier_cluster_algorithm.title: Outlier Cluster Algorithm

outlier_n_clusters.description: Expected number of clusters. None = auto-detect.
outlier_n_clusters.title: Outlier N Clusters

duplicate_flags.description: Hash flag groups for duplicate detection. None = DataEval default (hash_basic: xxhash + phash + dhash).
duplicate_flags.title: Duplicate Flags

duplicate_merge_near.description: Merge overlapping near-duplicate groups from different detection methods.
duplicate_merge_near.title: Duplicate Merge Near

duplicate_cluster_sensitivity.description: Threshold for cluster-based near duplicate detection (requires extractor). None = skip cluster detection.
duplicate_cluster_sensitivity.title: Duplicate Cluster Sensitivity

duplicate_cluster_algorithm.description: Clustering algorithm for cluster-based duplicate detection.
duplicate_cluster_algorithm.title: Duplicate Cluster Algorithm

duplicate_n_clusters.description: Expected number of clusters for duplicate detection. None = auto-detect.
duplicate_n_clusters.title: Duplicate N Clusters

health_thresholds.description: Warning thresholds for dataset health status. Findings are flagged as warnings.

DataCleaningWorkflowConfig.title: DataCleaningWorkflowConfig

DatasetProtocolConfig.description:
Dataset Configuration schema for an in-memory dataset.

Not serializable — for programmatic use only. Cannot be loaded from
YAML/JSON config files or edited in the builder UI.
DatasetProtocolConfig.title: DatasetProtocolConfig

DriftDetectorDomainClassifier.description:
Binary domain classifier (LightGBM) approach.

Trains a binary classifier to distinguish reference from test data.
An AUROC significantly above 0.5 indicates the distributions are
distinguishable — i.e. drift has occurred.

threshold.description: AUROC threshold above which drift is declared.
threshold.title: Threshold

classwise.description: Run drift detection per class for this detector. Requires labelled datasets. Runs non-chunked only.
classwise.title: Classwise

chunking.description: Chunked (temporal) drift analysis for this detector. None disables chunking.

DriftDetectorDomainClassifier.title: DriftDetectorDomainClassifier

DriftDetectorKNeighbors.description:
K-nearest neighbors distance-based drift detection.

Computes per-sample k-NN distances for test data against the reference
set. A Mann-Whitney U test determines whether test distances are
stochastically larger than reference self-distances.

p_val.description: Significance level for the Mann-Whitney U test.
p_val.title: P Val

classwise.description: Run drift detection per class for this detector. Requires labelled datasets. Runs non-chunked only.
classwise.title: Classwise

chunking.description: Chunked (temporal) drift analysis for this detector. None disables chunking.

DriftDetectorKNeighbors.title: DriftDetectorKNeighbors

DriftDetectorMMD.description:
Maximum Mean Discrepancy with permutation test.

Computes the MMD² statistic between reference and test distributions
using an RBF kernel, then estimates a p-value via permutation testing.

p_val.description: Significance level for the permutation test.
p_val.title: P Val

n_permutations.description: Number of permutations for the test.
n_permutations.title: N Permutations

device.description: PyTorch device for kernel computation (e.g. 'cpu', 'cuda:0'). None = auto.
device.title: Device

classwise.description: Run drift detection per class for this detector. Requires labelled datasets. Runs non-chunked only.
classwise.title: Classwise

chunking.description: Chunked (temporal) drift analysis for this detector. None disables chunking.

DriftDetectorMMD.title: DriftDetectorMMD

DriftDetectorUnivariate.description:
Univariate statistical test per feature.

Applies a chosen statistical test independently to each feature dimension
and flags drift when enough features reject the null hypothesis (after
multiple-testing correction).

test.description: Statistical test to apply per feature. 'ks' (Kolmogorov-Smirnov) is a good general-purpose default. 'cvm' (Cramér-von Mises) integrates squared CDF distance. 'mwu' (Mann-Whitney U) is rank-based and outlier-robust. 'anderson' (Anderson-Darling) is tail-sensitive. 'bws' (Baumgartner-Weiss-Schindler) has high power with tail sensitivity.
test.title: Test

n_features.description: Expected number of features. None = infer from reference data.
n_features.title: N Features

classwise.description: Run drift detection per class for this detector. Requires labelled datasets. Runs non-chunked only.
classwise.title: Classwise

chunking.description: Chunked (temporal) drift analysis for this detector. None disables chunking.

DriftDetectorUnivariate.title: DriftDetectorUnivariate

any_drift_is_warning.description: Non-chunked mode: if any detector flags drift, the finding is a warning. Set to False to treat all non-chunked results as informational.
any_drift_is_warning.title: Any Drift Is Warning

chunk_drift_pct_warning.description: Chunked mode: percentage of chunks that must drift to trigger a warning.
chunk_drift_pct_warning.title: Chunk Drift Pct Warning

consecutive_chunks_warning.description: Chunked mode: number of consecutive drifted chunks that triggers a warning. Sustained drift is typically more concerning than sporadic drift.
consecutive_chunks_warning.title: Consecutive Chunks Warning

classwise_any_drift_is_warning.description: Classwise mode: if any class drifts for any detector, the finding is a warning.
classwise_any_drift_is_warning.title: Classwise Any Drift Is Warning

DriftHealthThresholds.title: DriftHealthThresholds

DriftMonitoringWorkflowConfig.description:
Typed workflow configuration for ``drift-monitoring``.

Inherits all fields from :class:`DriftMonitoringParameters` — no ``params``
nesting required.

Example YAML::

    workflows:
      - name: drift_knn
        type: drift-monitoring
        detectors:
          - method: kneighbors
            k: 10

mode.description: advisory: report only, preparatory: modify dataset
mode.title: Mode

detectors.description: List of drift detectors to run. At least one required.
detectors.title: Detectors

update_strategy.description: Reference-set update strategy (stubbed — not yet applied at runtime). Accepted in config to preserve forward compatibility.

health_thresholds.description: Warning thresholds for drift severity classification.

DriftMonitoringWorkflowConfig.title: DriftMonitoringWorkflowConfig

ExtractorConfig.description:
Named extractor definition.

Combines model type, model-specific parameters, optional preprocessor,
and batch size into a single named unit. The ``model`` field acts as a
discriminator for which model-specific parameters are relevant.

YAML examples::

    extractors:
      - name: resnet_extractor
        model: onnx
        model_path: "./resnet50.onnx"
        output_name: "flatten0"
        preprocessor: resnet_preprocess
        batch_size: 64

      - name: flat_extractor
        model: flatten

      - name: bovw_extractor
        model: bovw
        vocab_size: 1024
        batch_size: 32
ExtractorConfig.title: ExtractorConfig

PreprocessingStep.description:
Single preprocessing step.

Pass-through to torchvision.transforms.v2 - any transform name is allowed.
See: https://pytorch.org/vision/stable/transforms.html

Example
-------
>>> step = PreprocessingStep(step="Resize", params={"size": 256, "antialias": True})
>>> step = PreprocessingStep(step="Normalize", params={"mean": [0.485], "std": [0.229]})
PreprocessingStep.title: PreprocessingStep

PreprocessorConfig.description:
Named preprocessor pipeline configuration.

Steps can be torchvision transforms (serializable, YAML-configurable)
or arbitrary callables (programmatic only).
PreprocessorConfig.title: PreprocessorConfig

SelectionConfig.description:
Named selection pipeline configuration.

Similar to PreprocessorConfig - defines reusable selection pipelines.
SelectionConfig.title: SelectionConfig

SelectionStep.description:
Single selection step - pass-through to dataeval.selection.

See: https://dataeval.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/autoapi/dataeval/selection/index.html
SelectionStep.title: SelectionStep

SourceConfig.description:
Named source definition — bundles a dataset with an optional selection.

YAML example::

    sources:
      - name: cifar_train_subset
        dataset: cifar10_train
        selection: first_5k
SourceConfig.title: SourceConfig

TaskConfig.description:
Task/workflow configuration schema.

Tasks reference sources (dataset+selection bundles) and an optional
extractor (model+preprocessor+batch_size bundle) by name.

enabled.description: Whether this task is included when running the pipeline.
enabled.title: Enabled

cache_dir.description: Directory for disk-backed computation cache. When set, expensive computations (embeddings, metadata, hash stats) are cached to disk and reused across runs. In containers, point to a mounted volume (e.g. /cache).
cache_dir.title: Cache Dir

TaskConfig.title: TaskConfig

UpdateStrategyConfig.description:
Reference-set update strategy — **stubbed for future use**.

When online drift detection is implemented, this will control how the
reference distribution evolves after each prediction step.  Currently
accepted in configuration but not applied at runtime.

type.description: Update algorithm. 'last_seen': sliding window. 'reservoir_sampling': uniform random sample.
type.title: Type

n.description: Number of samples to retain in the updated reference set.
n.title: N

UpdateStrategyConfig.title: UpdateStrategyConfig

description:
Top-level pipeline configuration.

All sections use a define-once, reference-by-name pattern.
Sources compose datasets with optional selections; extractors
compose model type/params with optional preprocessors.
Tasks reference workflows, sources, and extractors by name.

extractors.description: Named extractor definitions (model type + params + optional preprocessor + batch_size)
extractors.title: Extractors

workflows.description: Named workflow configurations (type + params), referenced by tasks
workflows.title: Workflows

title: PipelineConfig
